commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
feat(model-3d): integrate splitFaceByPolygon into Rectangle tool

When drawing a rectangle on an existing HEMesh face, the face is now
split in-place rather than creating a separate thin box object. This
enables push-pull to work naturally on the inner rectangle face.
Falls back to thin box creation for ground plane or when split fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): add splitFaceByPolygon kernel function

Implements the core polygon-split algorithm that all drawing-on-face
tools (Rectangle, Circle, Arc, Polygon) will use. For each polygon edge,
extends it to an infinite line and splits all tracked faces sequentially,
then identifies the inner face by centroid proximity.

Also fixes a critical bug in splitFaceByLine where the t parameter passed
to splitEdge was not adjusted for shared edges whose stored half-edge
direction is opposite to the face's half-edge direction. This caused
splits on box faces (or any face with shared edges) to create vertices
at the wrong positions (1-t instead of t).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix NewsLog not loading: use action=getAll instead of action=getPage

The webapp was calling action=getPage which doesn't exist in WebApp.gs (v28).
The API only implements action=getAll with limit/offset pagination.

Changes:
- buildUrl(): action=getPage + page=N → action=getAll + offset=(page-1)*limit
- fetchData(): derive total_pages/has_next/has_prev from response or compute
  locally when the API doesn't return them explicitly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix depth tool: hide dots when inactive, stronger control point influence

- Control point dots only visible when depth tool is active
- Existing points can be dragged (grab within 5% radius)
- Depth shader influence widened (5x vs 20x gaussian falloff) and
  strengthened (0.85 vs 0.5 blend) so points visibly reshape the scene
- History snapshot pushed before depth edits

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix React #418 hydration mismatch on blog date formatting

Use explicit locale ('en-US') and format options for toLocaleDateString()
to ensure consistent output between server and client rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(pwa): use NetworkFirst for navigation so installed PWA always gets fresh HTML

precacheAndRoute's implicit NavigationRoute was serving cached index.html,
making the installed PWA show stale UI after deployments. Registering a
NavigationRoute with NetworkFirst before precacheAndRoute overrides this,
so navigation always fetches from the network with the precache as offline
fallback — matching browser-tab update behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix rate-limit fallback: return null for new sessions, improve fallbacks

The "hmm, let me think about that" hardcoded response was firing on 429
errors, making rate-limited new sessions look like broken AI responses.

- New sessions now return null reply + rateLimited flag so client can
  use its own fallback instead of fake AI text
- Regular messages get shorter "sorry, give me one sec" fallback
- Improved client-side static fallbacks to ask about the connection
  instead of flat statements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(core): add generic confidence calculation (15 tests)

Keyword-based content confidence scoring (0.0-1.0). Accepts custom
required and bonus keyword lists — consumers provide domain-specific
keywords (death terms, biography terms, etc).

feat(phase29): complete Phase 29 visual anchoring — close all gaps G-2 through G-11

## Technical Debt Closed (G-2 → G-11)

G-2: CrossFadeImage — interactive SVG path via DOMPurify + dangerouslySetInnerHTML
  - useInteractiveSvg.ts: fetch SVG → DOMPurify.sanitize({USE_PROFILES:{svg:true}}) → render
  - AbortController on unmount (memory-safe)

G-3: GIF last-frame pause after cross-fade
  - useCrossFadeGif.ts: setTimeout 350ms → swap src to transparent 1×1 data URI

G-4: Hebrew FTS dictionary in PostgreSQL GIN index
  - 0016_visual_anchoring.sql: to_tsvector('simple',...) → to_tsvector('hebrew',...)

G-5: searchVisualAssets GraphQL query
  - visual-anchor.graphql: VisualAssetSearchResult type + searchVisualAssets query
  - visual-anchor.resolver.ts + service: full implementation with FTS

G-6: visual-anchoring-instructor.spec.ts — 5 Playwright E2E tests (with toHaveScreenshot)
G-7: visual-anchoring-visual.spec.ts — 7 visual regression tests (toHaveScreenshot)
G-8: AssetPicker.test.tsx — 13 unit tests
G-9: VisualBottomSheet.test.tsx — 26 unit tests (mobile)

G-10: ResumeBanner.tsx — "Return to last place" banner
  - role=status, aria-live=polite, 8s auto-dismiss, clearTimeout on unmount
  - Integrated into UnifiedLearningPage

G-11: anchorDomMap rebuild optimization
  - useAnchorDetection.ts: domMap built once per anchor-list-change (useEffect)
  - Scroll handler only does lazy querySelector for missing elements
  - Fixes scalability concern at 500+ anchors

## Tests
- Web: 3750/3750 ✅
- Security: 865/865 ✅
- Subgraph-content: 1096/1096 ✅

commit.message:
Add linked list vs array quantitative analysis with (n,i,s) cost model

- Cost model parameterized by n (size), i (position), s (element size)
- Key insight: C_arr(n,i,s) grows linearly with s, C_LL(n,i,s) does not
- Breakeven analysis solving for n and s with concrete values
- slabinfo table showing real kernel struct sizes (128–11840 bytes)
- Benchmark SVGs: cross-platform (3 machines) and element size comparison
- Experimental validation at s = 4/32/128/512 bytes

fix: make ARIA Insights panel show real drafts instead of placeholders

The right panel used drafts[0] from the unsorted backend response, which
could be a placeholder draft showing "[Contact Name]" context. Now
useIntelDrafts() sorts placeholders to the end, matching the list view
ordering, so the panel always reflects the first real draft.

commit.message:
ID-026: Add streaming atomic writes (Store.open_atomic) (#131)

* ID-026: Add streaming atomic writes (Store.open_atomic)

Context manager yielding a writable file object backed by a temporary
location. On successful exit the file is atomically promoted; on
exception the temp artifact is cleaned up and the target path is
unchanged.

Per-backend strategies:
- Local: mkstemp + os.replace
- SFTP: .~tmp.* + posix_rename (with fallback)
- S3/S3-PyArrow: SpooledTemporaryFile + PUT
- Azure non-HNS: SpooledTemporaryFile + PUT
- Azure HNS: temp blob + DFS rename
- Memory: BytesIO buffer

RFC-0004 accepted. Spec 022-streaming-atomic-writes.md (SAW-001-015).
ext.observe maps open_atomic to on_write hook.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ID-026: Address PR #131 review -- yield boundary, test name, README table

Bug: Split Azure HNS open_atomic yield boundary so user exceptions aren't remapped by _errors(). Test: Rename misleading test_on_write_does_not_fire_on_error to test_on_write_fires_with_error_on_failure. Ripple: Add open_atomic row to README Store API table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: LocalBackend open_atomic yield boundary -- prevent user PermissionError remapping

Split setup/yield/commit so PermissionError catch only covers mkdir, mkstemp, and os.replace -- not user code inside the context manager.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: suppress cleanup exceptions in LocalBackend atomic writes

Wrap os.unlink in contextlib.suppress(OSError) in both write_atomic and open_atomic cleanup paths, matching the pattern used by SFTP/S3/Azure backends.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------
